spa.name: Spa-Francorchamps
spa.fun_fact: Eau Rouge into Raidillon is one of the most famous corner sequences in motorsport, climbing nearly 40 metres in a blind flick.

monza.name: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
monza.fun_fact: Known as the Temple of Speed, cars spend over 80 percent of a lap at full throttle here.

silverstone.name: Silverstone Circuit
silverstone.fun_fact: Built on a former WWII RAF bomber airfield. Maggotts-Becketts-Chapel is one of the fastest sequences in F1.

nurburgring_gp.name: Nurburgring GP-Strecke
nurburgring_gp.fun_fact: The modern GP circuit sits next to the legendary 20.8 km Nordschleife, often called the Green Hell.

suzuka.name: Suzuka International Racing Course
suzuka.fun_fact: One of the only figure-eight layouts in top-level motorsport, with the back straight crossing over the esses.

watkins_glen.name: Watkins Glen International
watkins_glen.fun_fact: The Glen hosted the US Grand Prix for 20 years and is still regarded as one of America's great road courses.

road_america.name: Road America
road_america.fun_fact: The Kink at Turn 11 is famously taken flat-out in top prototypes, with zero margin for error.

interlagos.name: Autodromo Jose Carlos Pace
interlagos.fun_fact: One of only a handful of grand prix circuits that runs anti-clockwise, known for dramatic weather swings.

cota.name: Circuit of the Americas
cota.fun_fact: Turn 1 is an uphill blind apex with a 40 metre climb from the grid, one of the steepest in F1.

daytona_road.name: Daytona International Speedway Road Course
daytona_road.fun_fact: The road course uses the 31-degree banking of the oval at the West Horseshoe and NASCAR tri-oval.
